episode: DOJ directive to dismiss the Eric Adams prosecution
institutional_question: Whether federal charging authority can be used to obtain an elected official's cooperation with executive policy while an unopposed dismissal leaves the court with only narrow remedial power
primary_source_name: Acting Deputy Attorney General dismissal memorandum
notes: The district court ultimately dismissed with prejudice while criticizing the stated rationale; distinguish prosecutorial discretion from any claimed bargain.

episode: Attempted dismantling of Voice of America and associated broadcast networks
institutional_question: Whether an executive reduction order and administrative nonuse can nullify congressionally created and funded broadcasting functions
notes: Congress had appropriated funds and assigned functions; later appellate and merits developments require disposition normalization.

episode: Foreign-assistance freeze and attempted withholding of appropriated funds
institutional_question: Whether the executive may suspend or terminate foreign assistance that Congress appropriated and whether threshold or remedial rulings leave the power-of-the-purse issue unresolved
notes: Separate the initial blanket freeze from later contract terminations and any congressionally approved rescission.

episode: Removal of inspectors general without the statutory notice and reasons process
institutional_question: Whether removal notice requirements are enforceable when a court finds a statutory violation but does not reinstate the affected watchdogs
notes: The remedial outcome is more important to ARRP than the already identified notice violation.

episode: Executive orders and negotiated concessions targeting major law firms
institutional_question: Whether presidential control of clearances contracts buildings and agency access can be used to punish or extract concessions from legal advocates
primary_source_name: Paul Weiss presidential action and rescission record
notes: Four firms obtained constitutional rulings; agreements with other firms raise a separate coercion and review-evasion question.

episode: University funding freezes tied to governance curriculum admissions and hiring demands
institutional_question: Whether unrelated or insufficiently adjudicated federal funding leverage can compel institutional policy changes outside the grant programs' terms
primary_source_name: Harvard amended complaint and attached demand record
notes: Do not collapse legitimate civil-rights enforcement into the broader question of unrelated coercive conditions.

episode: Restriction of Associated Press access over editorial terminology
institutional_question: Whether discretionary presidential press access can be withdrawn to coerce editorial speech and whether existing relief remains durable on appeal
primary_source_name: Executive Order 14172 and AP access litigation record
notes: The order renamed the geographic feature; the alleged retaliation arose from enforcement of the administration's terminology demand.

episode: Pentagon newsroom eviction and outlet rotation
institutional_question: Whether access to permanent Defense Department press facilities can be allocated on viewpoint-neutral and reviewable terms
primary_source_name: Pentagon press-rotation memorandum described by both reports
notes: The two reports corroborate the underlying rotation; retrieve the signed Pentagon memorandum before publication use.

episode: FCC investigations involving broadcast and media companies criticized by the President
institutional_question: Whether an independent regulator's licensing and enforcement tools can be deployed in response to presidential grievances or editorial content
primary_source_name: FCC public notices and complaints collection
notes: Code each investigation separately before drawing a motive or retaliation inference.

episode: Executive order directing the end of federal funding for NPR and PBS
institutional_question: Whether the executive can terminate or condition congressionally structured public-media funding before or apart from a subsequent act of Congress
notes: Later congressional defunding may be a political failure and does not retroactively answer the executive-authority question.

episode: Presidential direction to investigate ActBlue fundraising
institutional_question: Whether the President may direct criminal civil and regulatory scrutiny toward a named opposing-party fundraising platform without neutral predicates and independent initiation safeguards
notes: The memorandum identifies broader contribution concerns but expressly foregrounds ActBlue; do not assume the underlying allegations are false.

episode: Presidential memoranda directing review of Chris Krebs and Miles Taylor
institutional_question: Whether individualized presidential directives can trigger clearance contract or agency investigations as retaliation for protected speech or prior service
primary_source_name: Presidential memoranda concerning Krebs and Taylor
notes: Retrieve and separately code both memoranda and any resulting agency action.

episode: Schedule Policy/Career reclassification of career employees
institutional_question: Whether large-scale loss of civil-service removal protections permits partisan control or retaliation within career functions
notes: HOR-011 already integrated this issue; retain as a corroborated manifestation and source update.

episode: Private dinner access tied to purchases of the President's meme coin
institutional_question: Whether anonymous or foreign purchasers can confer private financial benefit in exchange for presidential access without disclosure consent or enforceable conflict controls
primary_source_name: House Judiciary inquiry and source appendix
notes: Official inquiry is not proof of a violation; blockchain and beneficial-owner evidence should anchor any specific foreign-source claim.

episode: Trump Media transactions with crypto firms subject to federal regulation
institutional_question: Whether presidential financial interests and official regulatory policy create unmanaged or undisclosed conflicts involving regulated counterparties
notes: Do not infer an official quid pro quo from temporal proximity alone.

episode: Acceptance of a Qatari aircraft for presidential use with planned library transfer
institutional_question: Whether routing an extraordinary foreign-government benefit through the Defense Department and a presidential-library organization evades constitutional statutory disclosure or ownership safeguards
primary_source_name: Senate request for Defense Department inquiry
notes: The government-to-library transaction documents are more probative than public descriptions of the planned transfer.

episode: DOGE authority and conflicts involving Musk-regulated or government-contractor businesses
institutional_question: Whether a special government employee or informal operational leader can direct actions affecting agencies that regulate or contract with his companies without ordinary appointment disclosure and recusal controls
primary_source_name: State attorneys general complaint concerning DOGE authority
notes: Separate appointment authority data access contract cancellation and personal financial conflicts rather than treating DOGE as one issue.

episode: Use of Signal for senior-official discussion of Yemen strike plans
institutional_question: Whether ephemeral consumer messaging by senior officials defeats classified-information records-preservation and accountable war-decision processes
notes: The exact classification status was disputed; records-preservation and use-of-system questions do not depend entirely on classification.

episode: Executive direction over Smithsonian historical and cultural content
institutional_question: Whether the President may use funding and appointment influence to control content at a congressionally structured trust institution without clear statutory standards
notes: Congressional structure appropriations and Board of Regents authority must be examined before treating curatorial policy as an institutional failure.

episode: Blanket clemency for January 6 defendants including violent and seditious-conspiracy offenders
institutional_question: Whether any repairable transparency conflict or self-protection weakness exists despite the Constitution's broad pardon power
notes: The unpopular or sweeping exercise of a constitutional power is not itself an institutional defect; test disclosure self-protection and obstruction boundaries only.

episode: Conditioning counterterrorism grants on state election-policy changes
institutional_question: Whether unrelated public-safety grants may be conditioned on state compliance with executive election directives without clear congressional authorization
primary_source_name: FEMA grant conditions described in agency communications
notes: Already incorporated into federalism and election work; preserve as a high-value primary-document retrieval task.

episode: Threats to withhold federal funds from sanctuary jurisdictions
institutional_question: Whether the executive may impose broad immigration-cooperation conditions on unrelated federal grants without congressional authorization or germaneness
notes: ARRP does not take a position on sanctuary policy; the institutional question is funding authority and federalism.

episode: Sanctions and pressure intended to isolate or dismantle the International Criminal Court
institutional_question: Whether emergency economic authorities can supply a sanctions policy that Congress considered but did not specifically enact
notes: HOR-030 already placed the institutional defect in emergency-powers authority rather than ICC membership policy.

episode: Reduction of Bears Ears and Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monuments
institutional_question: Whether the Antiquities Act implicitly permits a successor President to materially diminish an established monument absent an act of Congress
notes: HOR-031 already pauses remedy development pending the Garfield County remand and challenges to the 2026 proclamations.

episode: Removal or displacement of multiple inspectors general after oversight activity
institutional_question: Whether presidential removal of watchdogs can neutralize pending oversight when notice requirements lack meaningful cause disclosure or timely remedies
primary_source_name: House hearing on the State Department Inspector General removal
notes: Use the first-term sequence as recurrence evidence; distinguish removals acting-designation changes and temporary displacement.

episode: Creation of Schedule F for policy-influencing career positions
institutional_question: Whether an executive classification can remove ordinary civil-service protections from a broad class of career positions
notes: The reporting is retrospective but independently corroborates the first-term order and its second-term continuation.

episode: Government political and pardon-seeker spending at Trump-owned properties
institutional_question: Whether federal and foreign-government patronage of presidential businesses can produce private benefit without adequate disclosure pricing and enforcement controls
primary_source_name: House Oversight report on presidential-property spending
notes: Use transaction-level records rather than assuming every official stay was directed or overpriced.

episode: Inaugural committee payments to the Trump International Hotel
institutional_question: Whether an inaugural nonprofit and presidential business can transact on non-arm's-length terms without adequate conflict and disclosure safeguards
notes: The civil case later settled without admission; test whether existing nonprofit campaign and inaugural rules already supply the remedy.

episode: Selection of Trump National Doral for the 2020 G7 summit and later reversal
institutional_question: Whether presidential control over event-site selection can direct public and foreign spending to a presidential business before review occurs
primary_source_name: Congressional Record concerning the Doral selection
notes: The selection was reversed before the summit; assess whether withdrawal avoided a durable merits or procurement ruling.

episode: White House and Commerce pressure surrounding NOAA's Hurricane Dorian statement
institutional_question: Whether political officials can compel or delay agency scientific communications without enforceable scientific-integrity and correction safeguards
notes: The Inspector General and scientific-integrity findings are the controlling substantiation; media labels should not replace the official findings.

episode: OMB withholding of congressionally appropriated Ukraine security assistance
institutional_question: Whether appropriated funds can be withheld for executive policy or political objectives when the Impoundment Control Act supplies no automatic corrective remedy
notes: GAO found an Impoundment Control Act violation; the remedy and enforcement gap is central to existing ARRP work.

episode: Family-separation implementation and failures to track and reunify families
institutional_question: Whether interagency enforcement policy may proceed without integrated identity tracking reunification duties and accountable implementation review
notes: Keep the policy merits separate from the institutional failures in planning tracking notice and reunification.

episode: Immediate removal of court-appointed United States attorneys
institutional_question: Whether presidential removal and acting-service maneuvers can nullify the judicial appointment backstop in 28 U.S.C. § 546(d) while the Executive avoids nominating a Senate-confirmed United States attorney
notes: Primary ownership is the consolidated APPT-001 appointments and confirmation-safeguards proposal. Do not presume the removals unlawful: 28 U.S.C. § 541(c), United States v. Hilario, and a 1979 OLC opinion support presidential removal authority. The possible defect is that removal plus acting-service mechanisms can defeat Congress's court-appointment and Senate-confirmation backstops. General Order 09-26 also documents the February 2025 removal of prior court-appointed U.S. Attorney Tessa Gorman without a confirmed successor. DOJ issue coverage should be added only if evidence connects a particular appointment maneuver to interference in a criminal matter or selective enforcement.
